stem: The table shows four operating system functions and the tasks they perform.
review_notes: 2024 J277/01 Q3(a). Match each function name to its task.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Match each operating system function to the task it performs.

right: Moves data from secondary storage to RAM
Installs device drivers and handles input/output devices
Allows the user to create, name and delete folders
Allows the user to interact with the computer via a GUI or command line
Stops unauthorised users from gaining access to the system

mark_points.text: Memory management — moves data from secondary storage to RAM
Peripheral management — installs device drivers and handles I/O
File management — create / name / delete folders
User interface — lets the user interact with the computer

misconceptions.label: File vs folder management
misconceptions.description: File management covers folders and files; 'folder management' alone is not the function name.
